what_it_does: Records ledger metadata for an image asset in the design-source channel, linking an id and address to a blob and labeling the file as design ground-truth.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, address, channel, path, blob, mime, name, dimensions, source, author_session, created, and empty links
body text reads 'SOURCE design ground-truth — deck1-2026/p-03.jpg'
id, address, path, and name all refer to deck1-2026/p-03
standouts: width and height are 0 for an image file
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for a ledger-style record
References a blob:// URI for the actual bytes
Tags the asset with source: design_source_material
concerns: width and height are 0, suggesting missing or unextracted image dimensions
No descriptive text, alt, or caption for the image
No links to related design files or deck context
notes: A sparse ledger stub for a design-source JPEG that identifies the asset and blob but leaves dimensions and relationships unset.
questions: Why are width and height 0 instead of the actual image dimensions?
What visual content does deck1-2026/p-03 contain?
Are there related deck pages or design files that should be linked?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to record design ground-truth for a deck image; the file does create a ledger record and blob pointer, but the zero dimensions and empty links make it an incomplete ground-truth stub.
intention_signals: To create a stable, retrievable record that marks a specific deck image as authoritative source material for the design system.
contribution: Provides a stable id/address and blob pointer plus a source classification for one image asset in the design-source channel.
summary_for_embedding: Ledger entry for image asset deck1-2026/p-03, an image/jpeg blob in the design-source channel tagged as design ground-truth with zero dimensions and no links.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a source image from deck1-2026 as retrievable, blob-addressed design ground-truth.
